info.title: Video API
info.description:
# Welcome to the Video API

This API provides powerful video and audio processing capabilities including:

- **Video Processing**: Cutting, transcoding, scaling, overlaying videos, and generating preview images
- **Audio Processing**: Transcoding, extracting, replacing, and mixing audio tracks; retrieve audio metadata
- **AI-Powered Features**: Text-to-speech generation with 30 natural voices; AI-based video sequence extraction, cutting, and full social media video composing
- **Rendering**: Subtitle rendering and custom overlay animations
- **File Management**: Uploading, downloading, and pulling files from external URLs with automatic cleanup

## Authentication

All API requests require authentication using an API key. Include your API key in the request header:

```
x-api-key: your-api-key-here
```

To obtain an API key:
1. Create a user account on https://video-api.io
2. Generate an API key for your user
3. Include the key in all your requests

## Credits System

Most operations consume credits from your account. Each endpoint's credit cost is documented
in its description. Monitor your credit balance in your user account on https://video-api.io

## Job-Based Processing

Many operations are asynchronous and return a Job ID. Poll the Job endpoint to check status
and retrieve results when processing is complete.

post.description: Transcode the video to a different format and resolution

Generates subtitles for that given video and renders them in the lower center of the video. To auto-correct spelling mistakes provide the optional script. If you need more control over where to place the subtitles, use the /render/subtitles endpoint and compose it via the /video/overlay endpoint.
Replaces the audio of the given video with the given audio
Overlays two or more videos/images over a background/base video
Mixes the audio into the given video file
Generates a preview image from the given video file
Extracts the audio from the given video file.
Generates a new video by cutting and merging the video parts as requested

Renders the given text as subtitles and burns them into the given video. The length of the resulting video is determined by the `lengthPrecedence` parameter: set to `VIDEO` to end at the video's natural end, or `SUBTITLE` to end when the last subtitle finishes.
Extracts video sequences by a given prompt from the given video
Creates a new video for social media by cutting sequences from the provided videos, adding voice-over, music and subtitles. The created video can directly be posted to social media.
Creates a new video by cutting sequences from the provided videos

x-cost-unit-based.unit: 60 seconds of video input (all input files are counted regardless of what the ai uses for the final video)

post.description: Renders subtitles for a given video file. Note that this is optimized currently for rendering of subtitles for portrait mode videos only!
Renders an overlay of one or multiple png layers that are animated based on the specified list of actions
Transitions a DRAFT job to SCHEDULED, performing a credit check and claiming a job slot.

402.description: Job slot limit reached or job allowance for the payment period exhausted

post.description: Uploads and temporarily stores a file for processing. Files are automatically deleted after a certain period of inactivity (or earlier when an expiresAt time is specified).
Downloads a file from an external URL and stores it for processing.

post.summary: Convert Audio to a Different Format
post.description: Converts audio data to a standard audio format (FLAC, AAC, AAC HQ or MP3)

Generates an audio clip of an AI voice reading your script (text-to-speech). You can also give instructions like 'read this slowly: ...', etc.

**Available voices:**

| Voice | Character |
|---|---|
| JILLIAN | Firm & confident |
| MARCUS | Firm & confident |
| ETHAN | Firm & confident |
| ANDREA | Youthful & energetic |
| OLIVER | Youthful & energetic |
| RYAN | Youthful & energetic |
| ELENA | Bright & cheerful |
| ZARA | Bright & cheerful |
| ALEX | Informative & clear |
| JAMES | Informative & clear |
| CLAIRE | Informative & clear |
| DAVID | Informative & clear |
| VICTOR | Informative & clear |
| DIANA | Smooth & flowing |
| GRACE | Smooth & flowing |
| NATALIE | Breezy & natural |
| MAYA | Breezy & natural |
| CHLOE | Easy-going & relaxed |
| JAMIE | Easy-going & relaxed |
| SOPHIA | Soft & gentle |
| LUNA | Soft & gentle |
| RICHARD | Mature & experienced |
| HENRY | Gravelly & mature |
| THOMAS | Even & mature |
| STELLA | Expressive & forward |
| EMMA | Friendly & approachable |
| SARAH | Warm & welcoming |
| RUBY | Lively & animated |
| ROSE | Gentle & kind |
| JAKE | Casual |

get.description: Retrieves metadata about a video file including resolution, duration, FPS, and frame count
Returns all jobs for the current user in a paginated way, ordered by scheduling time descending. Optionally filter by status.
Returns all information related to the given job including status, result, error messages, and credit costs

delete.description: Deletes a DRAFT job owned by the current user. Only jobs in DRAFT status can be deleted.

get.description: Downloads a file by ID via dlToken
Retrieves metadata about a stored file including filename, size, content type, and upload timestamp.
Downloads multiple files as a ZIP archive (STORE compression). All requested files must belong to the authenticated user.

get.summary: Download Multiple Files as ZIP (via dlToken)
get.description: Downloads multiple files as a ZIP archive (STORE compression). All requested files must belong to the authenticated user.
Get a short-lived file download token to download a file
Retrieves metadata about an audio file including duration

outputWidth.description: width of the output video file, set to 0 to keep current value, set to -1 to auto-calculate based on the aspect ratio

outputHeight.description: height of the output video file, set to 0 to keep current value, set to -1 to auto-calculate based on the aspect ratio

outputFps.description: frames per second (fps) of the resulting video, set to 0 to keep the fps of the source video

errorReason.description: did any error occur? If so, this will be anything other than NONE

error.description: in case of an error this will display the human readable error message

result.description: in case the job was successful, this will contain the id of the resulting file

paidCredits.description: the amount of credits charged for this job

estimatedCosts.description: the estimated cost in credits for this job (calculated at scheduling time)

outputFormat.description: output format for audio and video of the resulting video

style.description: style of the subtitle to render

script.description: script (optional, used to auto-correct spelling in subtitles)

videoFileId.description: video file to replace the audio of

backgroundVideoFileId.description: video id of the background/base video

overlays.description: list of overlays to put on top of the background

audioVolume.description: the volume of the audio from the audio file (between 0 and 100)

videoFileId.description: video file to create previews of

offset.description: offset (in seconds) to start taking previews from

offsetBetweenImages.description: offset (in seconds) between two preview images

targetWidth.description: width of the resulting preview images (height is calculated automatically to keep the original's video aspect ratio)

maxAmount.description: max amount of preview images to take

outputType.description: result type, either a stitched JPG consisting of all the preview images, or a zip file containing all the preview images

outputFps.description: frames per second (fps) of the resulting video

outputFormat.description: output format for audio and video of the resulting video

sequences.description: sequences of videos to cut into the final video

outputFormat.description: output format for audio and video of the resulting video

subtitle.description: subtitle text to burn into the video

subtitleSpeedFactor.description: subtitle text speed factor (higher = faster, lower = slower)

subtitleStyle.description: style of the subtitle to render

videoFileId.description: id of the video to extract sequences of

prompt.description: extraction prompt; something like 'cut the video based on the transition points'

outputFps.description: frames per second (fps) of the resulting video

outputFormat.description: output format for audio and video of the resulting video

items.description: video files to consider to cut sequences from into the final video

script.description: the script for the narrated voice-over (may contain stage directions)

voice.description: the voice for the voice-over, or null to not have a voice over

additionalPrompt.description: optional additional prompt for how to cut & compose the video

subtitleStyle.description: style of the subtitle to render, set to null for no subtitles

music.description: let the AI automatically choose fitting music and mix it in?

enableSmartCrop.description: when true, the AI will determine the optimal crop position for aspect ratio changes instead of center cropping

outputFps.description: frames per second (fps) of the resulting video

outputFormat.description: output format for audio and video of the resulting video

items.description: video files to consider to cut sequences from into the final video

prompt.description: prompt for the ai to know how the final video should look like

enableSmartCrop.description: when true, the AI will determine the optimal crop position for aspect ratio changes instead of center cropping

fileId.description: audio file or video file for which the subtitle should be generated for
